name: MCP Forge — MCP Server Template & Scaffolding
what_it_does: MCP Forge scaffolding tool and template system for creating new MCP servers — providing project templates, code generation, and bootstrapping utilities to quickly create well-structured MCP servers with Go, reducing boilerplate and helping developers follow MCP protocol best practices from the start.
use_cases: Scaffolding new MCP server projects from a standard template from developers
Generating boilerplate MCP server code with correct protocol structure
Following MCP best practices in new server development
Creating Go-based MCP servers with minimal setup overhead
Standardizing MCP server structure across development teams
Learning MCP server development patterns from scaffold examples
not_for: Running as an MCP server itself (it's a scaffolding/template tool)
Teams already with established MCP server patterns
best_when: A Go developer needs to quickly bootstrap a new MCP server with correct structure, protocol handling, and best practices built in.
avoid_when: You're not building MCP servers, or need Python/TypeScript scaffolding — use appropriate language-specific MCP SDKs.

auth.notes: CLI tool — no authentication needed. Used locally for code generation.

performance.rate_limits: Local CLI tool — no rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: This is a scaffolding tool, not an MCP server itself — cannot be used directly as an MCP
Go toolchain required to use generated templates
Template structure may not match latest MCP spec version
Community project — may lag behind MCP protocol updates
Generated code requires customization — not a zero-configuration solution
Review generated code before use — templates may have opinionated patterns
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local CLI code generation. No network access. No credentials. Generated code should be reviewed before deployment. Community tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local code generation only. No network. Review generated code. Community tool.
